what: Re-test the real split-8 branch with the stronger 4x32 reduction, restored quant hints, and the lower large-K waves_per_eu=1 hint from v310
why: v310 was the best recent benchmark on the repaired split-K path. The real split-8 branch was only tried at waves=2; lowering occupancy may recover register headroom without reintroducing the odd-partial masking that hurt some split-K=7 variants.

what: Retile the best split-K=7 waves=1 branch to a 4x64 reduction
why: v310 suggests the smaller reduction row tile matters, but every 4-row experiment so far also narrowed the width to 32. Restoring BSN=64 while keeping REDUCE_BSM=4 may preserve the better row-level parallelism with less reduce-grid fragmentation and fewer stores/launches.

what: Combine the real split-8 main kernel with a 4x64 reduction and waves_per_eu=1
why: v326 tests whether real split-8 benefits from the lower waves hint on the denser 4x32 reduce tile, and v327 tests whether 4x64 helps the best split-K=7 branch. This fills the missing corner by checking whether split-8 needs the wider 64-column reduction tile to offset its higher partial count.

what: Retile the split-K=7 quant-hints branch to a 4x64 reduction and let the compiler choose waves
why: v327 isolates the 4x64 geometry on the proven split-K=7 path with waves=1, but 4x64 changes the reduction occupancy enough that the explicit waves hint may no longer be best. The 4x32 family showed wave-sensitivity, so this is the clean follow-up inside the likely-valid branch.

what: Retile the split-K=7 quant-hints branch to a 4x64 reduction with waves_per_eu=2
why: The new 4x64 reduction geometry changes the occupancy balance enough that the old waves optimum from 4x32 may not transfer. Staging the high-occupancy endpoint alongside v327 and v329 gives a complete local sweep on the otherwise proven split-K=7 path.

what: Retile the split-K=7 quant-hints branch to an 8x64 reduction with waves_per_eu=1
why: The new 4x64 sweep widens the reduction while keeping the more parallel four-row tile, but older work showed BSM=8 can help the reduce phase by doubling the row blocks without paying the very fine-grained launch overhead of BSM=4. Applying that older reduce-only idea to the current quant-hints branch is still untested.

what: Retile the split-K=7 quant-hints branch to an 8x64 reduction and let the compiler choose waves
why: v327 shows the 4x64 family regresses, which makes the coarser 8x64 reduction the more plausible reduce-width follow-up. The 8x64 branch is now under test at waves=1; staging the waves=0 point prepares the adjacent occupancy setting if explicit waves are no longer optimal on the wider reduction.

what: Retile the split-K=7 quant-hints branch to an 8x64 reduction with waves_per_eu=2
why: v331 shows the 8x64 reduction is valid and competitive at 8.64 us BM. To finish the occupancy sweep on this coarser reduction geometry, the remaining untested endpoint is the higher explicit waves hint, which may work better now that the reduce stage is less fragmented than 4x64.

what: Keep the v331 split-K=7 8x64 waves=1 branch and remove the fused quant stride/contiguous hints
why: v331 is valid and competitive at 8.64 us BM, but the same hint bundle has been volatile across nearby split-K branches: it helped the 4x32 winner yet some hint-heavy variants regressed badly. On the coarser 8x64 reduction, the launch/ILP balance changed enough that the quant hints may now be neutral or harmful. This isolates that interaction while keeping the new best reduce geometry fixed.

what: Retile the split-K=7 branch to a 16x128 reduction with waves_per_eu=1
why: The 8x64 family is valid and competitive, while 4x64 regressed badly. The remaining nearby reduce-only direction is to coarsen further, cutting the reduce grid in half again. Older work touched 16x128 before the current split-K branch family existed, so this tests whether the newer main-kernel path benefits from a lower-overhead reduce stage.

what: Retile the split-K=7 branch to a 16x128 reduction and let the compiler choose waves
why: v334 isolates the coarser 16x128 reduction with the same waves=1 setting that won the 8x64 sweep, but the occupancy balance changes again when the reduce grid is halved. The natural adjacent control is to remove the explicit waves hint and see whether the compiler picks a better residency for the even coarser reduction stage.

what: Retile the split-K=7 branch to a 16x128 reduction with waves_per_eu=2
why: v334 is valid but benchmark-gated, and v335 already covers compiler-chosen residency. To complete the local occupancy sweep on the coarser 16x128 reduction family, the remaining endpoint is the higher explicit waves hint.
